source_jobs.title: Client Strategy Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Client Strategy Lead to play a pivotal role in owning client relationships, driving integrated strategy across disciplines (creative, media, CRO), and championing business growth for our clients - and for Pearmill.

You’ll be the connective tissue between our internal teams and our clients - ensuring we’re not only meeting expectations, but identifying new opportunities, surfacing risks early, and creating long-term impact.

This role is not channel- or craft-specific. You won’t be expected to write copy or optimize bids - but you will be expected to understand the dynamics of each and bring the pieces together into a single, compelling strategy.

This role reports into our CEO (for now), but is expected to grow into a leadership path within our client services org as we scale.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the client relationship end-to-end, building trust and delivering strategic value across creative, media, and CRO.

 - Lead the strategic vision across all disciplines for your accounts - ensuring we’re driving measurable outcomes, not just completing tasks.

 - Synthesize input from craft teams (e.g. Paid Social, Creative, Search, CRO) into a cohesive client-facing strategy.

 - Facilitate QBRs and strategic check-ins with clients - bringing thought-starters, insights, and cross-functional POVs to the table.

 - Identify risks and opportunities early - whether it’s signs of churn or whitespace for upsell - and work with internal partners to act on them.

 - Drive internal alignment across media, creative, and CRO so that the whole team is rowing in the same direction.

 - Be a retention engine. You are the heartbeat of the account - ensuring we’re meeting goals, exceeding expectations, and expanding the relationship over time.

 - Lead or co-lead new client pitches translating agency capabilities into tailored proposals that win new business.

 - Maintain awareness of industry dynamics and emerging trends, providing forward-thinking insights to both clients and internal teams.


ABOUT YOU

 - You have 7+ years of experience in performance marketing, strategy, client services, or integrated account management.

 - You’re a business systems thinker who can zoom out and connect the dots - across data, creative, and human dynamics.

 - You've demonstrated success in growing client relationships and expanding scope beyond initial engagements.

 - You're a confident, consultative communicator who can be respectfully confrontational when needed to drive better outcomes.

 - You’re proactive, curious, and solutions-oriented - you don’t just flag problems, you propose next steps.

 - You have experience managing integrated marketing strategies across multiple channels (paid search, paid social, etc.).

 - You can translate ambiguity into action - bringing structure, clarity, and momentum to a situation.


WHAT YOU’LL FIND HERE

 - Competitive salary with bonus structure tied to client satisfaction, retention, and growth

 - 99% coverage for health, dental, and vision

 - Distributed, remote, and global culture

 - Unlimited vacation with 3-weeks required vacation

 - Unlimited mental health or physical health leave

 - Remote work budget to make it easier to work from wherever you feel most productive!

 - Monthly physical activity budget to get your body moving

 - 16 weeks of fully paid parental leave

 - Stipend for laptop and other home office equipment

 - Annual indoor plant budget

 - Annual company-wide retreat

 - Yearly stipend for books, seminars, courses, and coaches

 - Matching for charitable donations

 - Clear career progression path toward senior client leadership roles




DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION

Pearmill is a diverse company with team members hailing from all backgrounds and walks of life. We’re a globally-distributed team and committed to continuing to build a company that represents the world we live in. We encourage candidates of underrepresented groups to apply.

source_jobs.title: Paid Social Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Paid Social Manager, you'll work closely with our growth & creative teams to plan, launch, and optimize campaigns across Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn, and other social platforms. You'll be responsible for executing campaigns, monitoring performance, and testing new ideas to drive growth for our clients.

This is a hands-on role, ideal for someone who has some experience in paid social and wants to deepen their skills in a fast-paced agency environment. You'll get exposure to diverse industries, work directly with clients, and collaborate with senior team members who will support your growth.

You’ll join a collaborative paid social team that supports each other, brainstorms together, and continually improves how we work. As Pearmill grows, you’ll have opportunities to expand your responsibilities and make a meaningful impact on the business.


WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own end-to-end paid social strategy and execution for a portfolio of performance marketing clients.

 - Build media plans and manage budgets, pacing, forecasting, and ongoing optimizations.

 - Launch, manage, and iterate campaigns across platforms like Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn, Reddit, Snapchat, and other emerging channels when there is a clear testing rationale.

 - Drive a structured experimentation program for each of your clients, defining hypotheses and tests, and documenting learnings and next steps.

 - Partner with creative, landing page, and tech teammates to develop and QA assets, troubleshoot performance or tracking issues, and improve conversion rates.

 - Communicate clearly with clients to align on strategy, performance goals, and results.


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of hands-on paid social experience in a performance environment (agency experience is a plus).

 - Experience managing budgets over $300k a month.

 - Deep expertise in Meta Ads Manager (planning, building, troubleshooting, and scaling).

 - Comfort managing multiple campaigns and priorities at once, while still maintaining QA discipline.

 - Strong analytical ability:
   
   - can use data to find patterns and diagnose issues
   
   - can turn insights into clear actions.

 - Understand attribution models and incrementality testing.

 - Clear writing and communication skills (especially helpful in async environments).

 - +/- 2 hours of ET required.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience running paid social for B2B, lead gen, or long cycle products.

 - Have worked with start-ups and SMBs before and understand the differences in their strategies.


BENEFITS

 - Distributed and global culture.

 - Remote work budget and equipment budget.

 - Learning budget (books, courses, coaches).

 - Share in profits.

 - Unlimited vacation (3 weeks required).

 - And more.




DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION

Pearmill is a diverse company with team members hailing from all backgrounds and walks of life. We’re a globally-distributed team and committed to continuing to build a company that represents the world we live in. We encourage candidates of underrepresented groups to apply.

source_jobs.title: Analytics Lead
source_jobs.description:
About the company

You've never worked for a company quite like Pearmill. We're a bunch of eclectic folks paving our own path in the world of technology, marketing, and advertising.

We're a diverse group of designers, engineers, and marketers from across the world, working together to help grow challenger brands and fast-growing startups through art and technology.

Pearmill is a performance marketing agency that outperforms expectations, using data-driven insights and relentless innovation for exponential growth.

About the role

We're looking for an Analytics Lead to own the full measurement stack — from the pixels firing on a client's checkout page to the incrementality test that tells us whether that Meta campaign is actually moving the needle. This is a player-coach role: you'll set the measurement philosophy and learning agenda for the agency, manage a junior tracking specialist who handles hands-on QA and implementation, and work directly with client teams to translate data into decisions.

This role sits at the intersection of analytics engineering, measurement science, and performance strategy. You won't just build dashboards — you'll design the tests that prove whether our work is working.


WHAT YOU'LL OWN


TRACKING & DATA INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Own the end-to-end tracking architecture across all client accounts: Google Tag Manager, GA4, CDPs (Segment, RudderStack, Teleaum, etc.), Meta Pixel + CAPI, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, TikTok Events API, and any other ad network data pipelines — and be well versed enough in these systems to support clients hands-on with implementation

 - Define tagging standards, naming conventions, and data layer specifications for new client onboarding

 - Review and sign off on all pixel/tag implementations done by the junior tracking specialist before anything goes live

 - Lead tracking QA processes: diagnose misfires, validate conversion events, and ensure data integrity across environments (dev, staging, production)

 - Own server-side tracking setups and consent-mode configurations as privacy requirements evolve


MEASUREMENT FRAMEWORKS & STRATEGY

 - Build measurement plans for every client: define the KPIs that actually matter, the attribution approach best suited to their funnel, and the testing roadmap for the next 6–12 months

 - Design and run incrementality tests — geo-based holdouts, PSA holdout tests, conversion lift studies — and translate results into media budget recommendations

 - Interpret and pressure-test platform-reported metrics (Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, etc.); challenge results that don't hold up to scrutiny

 - Lead or collaborate on Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM) engagements — either scoping vendor-run models or building lightweight versions in-house

 - Own the cross-channel attribution model: evaluate last-click vs. data-driven vs. time-decay approaches per client context and document your rationale


REPORTING & INSIGHTS

 - Build and maintain executive-level dashboards (Looker Studio, Looker, Power BI, or equivalent) that go beyond vanity metrics to show incremental business impact

 - Establish a regular reporting cadence: weekly performance pulse, monthly deep-dives, and quarterly measurement reviews with clients

 - Write clear, opinionated insight narratives — not just charts — that connect data to the decisions clients need to make


TEAM & PRACTICE DEVELOPMENT

 - Manage one junior Analytics/Tracking Specialist: set their priorities, review their work, give them a clear growth path

 - Build the analytics practice playbook: onboarding templates, tracking specs, measurement plan frameworks, experiment design guides

 - Stay current on the ad platform measurement landscape — platform pixel changes, consent/privacy shifts, new incrementality products from Meta, Google, TikTok — and update internal practices accordingly


YOU'RE A STRONG FIT IF YOU HAVE

 - 4–7 years of experience in marketing analytics, measurement, or marketing science at a perfor

notes: Performance marketing agency with a mix of strategy, analytics, and paid social roles
